title: AI Fact or Fiction: The Fable Ban, Tokenmaxxing, Saaspocolypse — With Ara Kharazian
summary: Ara Kharazian, lead economist at Ramp, analyzes whether AI spending by companies is producing real value. The discussion examines market adoption trends, evaluates claims like "tokenmaxing" and the predicted SaaS apocalypse, and assesses competitive positions including Anthropic's rise relative to OpenAI and the resurgence of DeepSeek.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz hosts Ramp lead economist Ara Kharazian to test whether corporate AI spending is producing real value, using a fact-or-fiction format. The conversation reviews market adoption trends and weighs popular claims, including "tokenmaxing" and the predicted SaaS apocalypse. It also assesses competitive standings, covering Anthropic's rise relative to OpenAI and the resurgence of DeepSeek. The framing matters because it pairs the economics of enterprise AdoptIon with specific industry narratives, separating measurable activity from speculation.
